channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: House Republican apologizes for wearing blackface at 2006 Halloween party
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) apologized late Thursday night for wearing blackface as part of a Halloween costume portraying pop star Michael Jackson in 2006 during a college party.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Obama will campaign with Harris in key swing states next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Barack Obama is planning to hit key swing states to boost Vice President Kamala Harris’ campaign for the White House, starting next Thursday in Pittsburgh. The Harris campaign says Obama will travel around the country over the final 27 days ahead of the election.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nigerian track star Oba Femi shines as NXT superstar
item.description: Femi will defend his title Tuesday night on the CW Network. It's a show that also features WWE legend Randy Orton.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, ABC 4

item.title: Sheriff says rescuers 'will not rest' as search for Helene's victims drags into second week
item.description: PENSACOLA, N.C. (AP) — The search for victims of Hurricane Helene dragged into its second week on Friday, as exhausted rescue crews and volunteers continued to work long days — navigating past washed out roads, downed power lines and mudslides — to reach the isolated and the missing.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina sets Nov. 1 execution as state ramps up use of death chamber
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's high court on Friday set a date of Nov. 1 to put to death a man who killed a store clerk a quarter-century ago, the second of an expected six executions in about six months as the state ramps up its use of capital punishment after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: Hurricane Helene’s storm surge struck Florida fast and hard when it slammed into Gulf Coast communities near St. Petersburg.
item.dc:creator: David Fischer And Terry Spencer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigrant murder rate 'tens of thousands' higher than ICE's bombshell figures: data expert
item.description: The total number of noncitizens in the U.S. who have murder convictions is likely "tens of thousands" more than the 13,400 listed on Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s national docket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Arkansas parents join thousands across US petitioning for metal detectors in schools
item.description: "At some point, a kid is going to get ahold of something, and a metal detector may help prevent them from getting it into a school," Destiny said.
item.dc:creator: Neale Zeringue, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most visited national park site closed indefinitely due to Hurricane Helene
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KRON4

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is your deadline?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Wawa named No. 1 convenience store in consumer satisfaction survey
item.description: Analysts noted that many of the top-ranked chains specialized in ready-to-eat foods prepared in the stores' kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KTLA 5

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Kirk strengthens into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Kirk strengthened Wednesday into a Category 3 storm in the Atlantic Ocean and was expected to grow rapidly into a major hurricane, forecasters said. There were no coastal watches or warnings in effect, and the storm system was not yet deemed a threat to land.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fed agencies released noncitizens without ID into US, allowed them to board domestic flights: DHS OIG report
item.description: A new DHS Inspector General report finds CBP, ICE, and TSA allowed noncitizens without ID to enter the U.S. and board domestic flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: Shock of deadly floods is a reminder of Appalachia’s risk from violent storms in a warming climate
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Hurricane Helene dumped trillions of gallons of water hundreds of miles inland, devastating communities nestled in mountains far from the threat of storm surge or sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly Maui fire erupted from earlier blaze believed to have been extinguished, investigation finds
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — The wildfire that killed at least 102 people on Maui last year erupted from an earlier brushfire, sparked by downed power lines, that firefighters believed they had extinguished, officials confirmed Wednesday as they presented their findings on the cause. The Aug.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CNN launches digital paywall for the first time
item.description: CNN, one of the most visited news sites in the world, announced Tuesday that visitors will soon have to pay to view the website. CNN.com will implement a paywall, asking visitors to pay $3.99 a month or a discounted rate of $29.99 a year to access its news content.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: 5 Chinese nationals charged with covering up midnight visit to Michigan military site
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — U.S. authorities charged five Chinese nationals with lying and trying to cover their tracks, more than a year after they were confronted in the dark near a remote Michigan military site where thousands of people had gathered for summer drills.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Houston teens carjack driver to smuggle migrants
item.description: Two men have pleaded guilty to a carjacking in South Texas where they said they stole a vehicle in order to smuggle migrants into the country.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Doctor who helped supply Matthew Perry ketamine pleads guilty to drug charge
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry ’s fatal overdose pleaded guilty Wednesday to conspiring to distribute the surgical anesthetic ketamine.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: After Helene cuts roads, power, phones, Southerners stay in touch the old-fashioned way
item.description: In remote mountain areas, helicopters hoisted the stranded to safety while search crews waded through floodwaters and moved toppled trees so they could look door to door for survivors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5
